articles.title: Tribal Students in Central Wyoming Release Small Fish in a Big Pond
articles.summary: RAY LAKE, Wyo. — There was a lot of giggling in the parking lot as teenagers plunged bare hands into coolers filled with small, slippery rainbow trout fry.  The objective was to catch the fish in clear plastic cups, but the juvenile trout were fast and very squirmy, and the effort elicited shrieks, splashing and […]
articles.claude_summary: Full read needed — The snippet establishes a scene of tribal students in central Wyoming releasing trout fry into a lake, suggesting a hands-on conservation or environmental education program, but the article's core findings, outcomes, or educational significance aren't clear from the excerpt alone.

articles.title: The Tulsa Race Massacre Happened 106 Years Today – Here Are Teaching & Learning Resources
articles.summary: Collection of the Smithsonian National Museum of African American History and Culture   The terrible Tulsa Race Massacre happened 106 years ago today. You might be interested in MAY 31ST & JUNE 1ST WERE THE DATES OF THE TULSA RACE MASSACRE – HERE ARE TEACHING & LEARNING RESOURCES.
articles.source_feed: Larry Ferlazzo's Websites of the Day…
articles.reason: Valuable teaching resources for critical history.
articles.claude_summary: Full read needed — This is a resource roundup marking the anniversary of the 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre, linking to Smithsonian NMAAHC materials and prior teaching collections. Classroom-ready for history, civil rights, or current events units.

articles.title: Artificial Intelligence Is Here To Stay. Are Hawaiʻi Schools Ready?
articles.summary: For many schools, it’s a race to keep up. Others are leading the pack. And some are unsure what to do with it. Artificial intelligence is playing an increasingly prominent role in Hawaiʻi education, from the recent opening of the state’s first AI-focused charter school to the development of new coursework teaching students how to […]
articles.reason: Concrete example of AI in schools, specific charter school.
articles.claude_summary: Full read needed — The snippet establishes that Hawaii schools are at varying stages of AI adoption — ranging from a new AI-focused charter school to districts still figuring out their approach — but doesn't reveal specific findings, data, or a clear argument beyond the framing.

articles.title: J.R.R. Tolkien on Fairy Tales, Language, the Psychology of Fantasy, and Why There’s No Such Thing as Writing “For Children”
articles.summary: "Creative fantasy, because it is mainly trying to do something else (make something new), may open your hoard and let all the locked things fly away like cage-birds."
articles.reason: Philosophical take on creative fantasy, language, and learning.
articles.claude_summary: Tolkien argues that fantasy is not escapism in the pejorative sense but a legitimate act of sub-creation — humans making new worlds as an extension of their nature as creatures made by a creator. Writing "for children" is a false category; if a story is good, it's good for everyone, and condescending to young readers reflects a failure of craft and honesty. Language and myth-making are inseparable: the invented word and the invented world co-arise, and fantasy at its best liberates imagination in ways realistic fiction cannot.

articles.title: Schools Are Spending $4 Billion Annually On SEL, Apparently Much Of It On Digital Platforms?  Give Me A Break….
articles.summary: Education Week recently published an article, Is Your School’s SEL Strategy Working? The Questions Every Educator Should Ask, which highlighted the above statistic. The article linked to two resources.  One is the Social and Emotional Learning Market Report 2026, which primarily talks about paying for digital resources. The other is a resource for “Evidence-Based […]
articles.source_feed: Larry Ferlazzo's Websites of the Day…
articles.reason: Critical look at SEL spending and effectiveness in schools.
articles.claude_summary: Full read needed — Ferlazzo pushes back on an Education Week claim that schools spend $4 billion annually on SEL, arguing the figure is driven by digital platform sales rather than reflecting genuine instructional investment in social-emotional learning. He's skeptical that vendor-driven spending data should define the SEL landscape.

articles.title: Wemby’s Post-Game Interview Is Made-To-Order For An SEL Lesson
articles.summary: I’ve been a New York Knicks fan for nearly sixty-years (I grew-up on Staten Island), but how can you not like Victor Wembanyama? After being responsible for throwing the ball away for a turnover, and then missing the final shot, he modeled what taking responsibility is like:   Which is why I’m add this info […]
articles.source_feed: Larry Ferlazzo's Websites of the Day…
articles.reason: Uses real-world example to teach responsibility for SEL.
articles.claude_summary: Wembanyama publicly modeled accountability after costing the Spurs a game—owning both the turnover and the missed final shot without deflection. Ferlazzo flags the post-game interview as a ready-made SEL teaching moment on taking responsibility.
